h1Finding.bestInterpretation: In the Medi-Cal context, "H1" is almost certainly a Medi-Cal AID CODE, not an immigration/visa term. On the California DHCS Aid Codes Master Chart, H1 is a full-scope, no-share-of-cost eligibility code in the infant/child poverty-level series (Infants ages 0-1 at roughly 200%-250% FPL; the H-series H1-H6 covers infants/children by age and FPL band). It is an internal eligibility classification a member would never see named as a 'barrier' - so 'issues like H1' most likely means paperwork/classification errors (e.g., being placed in a restricted vs. full-scope code) or a misremembered shorthand. The immigration reading (H-1B visa) was judged less likely because nobody shortens 'H-1B' to 'H1' as an access-barrier label.
h1Finding.evidence: Confirmed on the official DHCS/Medi-Cal Aid Codes Master Chart (aidcodes.pdf) and Aid-Code-Chart-2024.pdf, where H1-H6 are listed under full-scope benefits. No source supported a 'health-literacy code' or standalone 'hospital classification' meaning.
